articles.summary: Product teams are drowning in AI complexity: building models, managing inference, and scaling production. None of the off-the-shelf solutions understand your unique business context, and piecing together different tools kills productivity.

articles.title: Master AI Agents: Building Production-Ready Applications
AI AND PRODUCT MANAGEMENT: BECOMING MORE EVIDENCE-GUIDED
articles.summary: GenAI is often portrayed as a way for product managers to crank out documents and prototypes faster, but its true power is in enabling evidence-driven discovery. Used well, it can lighten the cognitive load of research and analysis while leaving empathy, collaboration, and human judgment where they belong - with people.

articles.title: IS THE INNOVATOR'S DILEMMA OUTDATED?
articles.summary: Big tech firms don't usually crush startups by copying them - small markets just aren't worth their effort. However, with AI labs, every model update spawns accidental products, collapsing the boundary between platform and application and changing how software, media, and even financial reporting get made and framed.

articles.title: THE PROBLEM WITH THE PERFECT ROADMAP
articles.summary: Polished roadmaps create false certainty, waste time, and discourage change. Useful roadmaps stay messy, flexible, and outcome-focused, guiding strategy through clarity, iteration, and honest acknowledgment of uncertainty.

articles.title: PROMOTIONS ARE BASED ON IMPACT, NOT JUST PEOPLE SKILLS
articles.summary: Advancing into management requires proving that added resources under your leadership will drive greater business impact. Success comes from expanding scope, building a strong business case, and cultivating strategic relationships.

articles.title: WHY 84% OF PMS FEAR FAILURE: AN ANALYSIS OF ATLASSIAN'S STATE OF PRODUCT 2026 REPORT
articles.summary: 1000+ product teams shared their experiences in Atlassian's State of Product 2026 report [10] - revealing that product work is under more scrutiny than ever, and the pressures it creates on PMs. Join the webinar to understand what these findings mean for the future of product management, and how you can prepare. Join live (this week!)

articles.title: HERO IMAGES ARE DEAD. THESE SOLUTIONS ARE REPLACING THEM
articles.summary: Full-width hero images often waste above-the-fold space, offering style over clarity. More effective alternatives - split layouts, text-first designs, or search-first approaches - prioritize speed, usability, and conversions. Testing is essential to validating impact.

articles.title: HOW TO BRIDGE THE AI EXPERIENCE GAP AND DRIVE FEATURE ADOPTION
articles.summary: Users can't judge AI features without firsthand experience, creating a value “experience gap.” Offer powerful trials first, then monetize with intuitive limits instead of confusing usage caps.

articles.title: VERTICAL VS. HORIZONTAL ORG COUPLING
articles.summary: Organizational effectiveness depends on balancing cohesion and coupling, adapting structures fluidly, and managing dependencies without assuming any state is inherently optimal.

articles.title: HOW TO NAIL YOUR NEXT BIG TALK
articles.summary: Public speaking confidence comes from clarity, not bravado. A structured framework helps leaders engage audiences, reduce nerves, and leave a lasting impact.

articles.title: WHY SMALL MODELS ARE THE FUTURE OF AGENTIC AI
articles.summary: Small models fit agentic AI tasks better and cost less, while mixed systems can handle broader conversations efficiently.

articles.title: COMPETITIVE RESEARCH
articles.summary: Competitive research goes beyond feature comparisons, offering insights into indirect rivals, target customers, strategy, and positioning.
